remember: Students will be able to recall and describe the various geomorphic processes such as weathering, erosion, and deposition.
Students will be able to identify and differentiate between different types of weathering processes.
Students will be able to explain the causes and effects of erosion and deposition in different landscapes.
understand: Students will be able to understand the importance of geomorphic processes in shaping the Earth's surface.
Students will be able to explain how geomorphic processes interact with each other and with the environment.
Students will be able to describe the role of geomorphic processes in creating and modifying landforms.
apply: Students will be able to apply their knowledge of geomorphic processes to explain the formation of different landforms and features.
Students will be able to use case studies to demonstrate the impact of geomorphic processes on human settlements and environments.
Students will be able to design and propose strategies for mitigating the effects of geomorphic processes on human populations.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of geomorphic processes on the environment and human societies.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of different strategies for mitigating the effects of geomorphic processes.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the geomorphic processes that occur in different regions and environments.

ncert_objectives: Students will be able to describe and explain the various geomorphic processes such as weathering, erosion, and deposition.
Students will be able to identify and explain the different types of weathering processes and their effects on the environment.
Students will be able to understand the role of geomorphic processes in shaping the Earth's surface and creating landforms.
board_exam_objectives: To test the student's knowledge of geomorphic processes and their effects on the environment.
To assess the student's ability to apply their knowledge of geomorphic processes to explain the formation of different landforms and features.
To evaluate the student's understanding of the importance of geomorphic processes in shaping the Earth's surface and creating landforms.
